Title: Arabidopsis nitrate reductase activity is stimulated by the E3 SUMO ligase AtSIZ1

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nitrate reductase; molybdoenzyme; plant NR homodimer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NAD(P)H-dependent reduction of nitrate to nitrite.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate required for ammonium production and nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen source for amino-acid pool and plant growth/development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Sumoylation of NIA1 by AtSIZ1 increases nitrate reductase activity, promoting nitrate reduction, nitric oxide production, and nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIA1 physically interacts with AtSIZ1 and is sumoylated by AtSIZ1 in vitro and in vivo. 2. The principal SUMO acceptor site is K356, and K356R blocks sumoylation. 3. Sumoylated NIA1 shows much higher nitrate reductase specific activity than non-sumoylated NIA1, while non-sumoylated NIA1 can still form homodimers. 4. Reduced NIA1 function in siz1-2 contributes to low nitrogen content, high nitrate content, low NO, and growth defects rescued by ammonium.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript detected in leaves and roots; analyzed in Arabidopsis plants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected in leaves and roots; transcript level increased in siz1-2 relative to WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ammonium versus nitrate supply; cycloheximide treatment; chlorate treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; XVE inducible promoter (for transient expression assays)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: siz1-2 mutant phenotyping; chlorate sensitivity assay; in vitro pull-down; yeast two-hybrid; in vitro sumoylation assay; in vivo transient expression and immunoprecipitation; K356R site-directed mutagenesis; nitrate reductase activity assay; specific activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of AtSIZ1-mediated activation is associated with dwarfism, early flowering, abnormal seed development, reduced NO, and impaired nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing NIA1 activity or its SUMO-dependent activation could improve nitrate assimilation efficiency, but manipulation should consider pleiotropic effects on flowering, growth, and defense signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both sumoylated and non-sumoylated NIA1 can form homodimers; protein stability is promoted by AtSIZ1. The paper treats ammonium rescue as evidence that defective nitrate reduction underlies the siz1 phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nitrate reductase; molybdoenzyme; plant NR homodimer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NAD(P)H-dependent reduction of nitrate to nitrite.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate required for ammonium production and nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen source for amino-acid pool and plant growth/development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Sumoylation of NIA2 by AtSIZ1 increases nitrate reductase activity, promoting nitrate reduction, nitric oxide production, and nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIA2 physically interacts with AtSIZ1 and is sumoylated by AtSIZ1 in vitro and in vivo. 2. The principal SUMO acceptor site is K353, and K353R blocks sumoylation. 3. Sumoylated NIA2 shows much higher nitrate reductase specific activity than non-sumoylated NIA2, while non-sumoylated NIA2 can still form homodimers. 4. Reduced NIA2 function in siz1-2 contributes to low nitrogen content, high nitrate content, low NO, and growth defects rescued by ammonium.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript detected in leaves and roots; analyzed in Arabidopsis plants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected in leaves and roots; transcript level increased in siz1-2 relative to WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ammonium versus nitrate supply; cycloheximide treatment; chlorate treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; XVE inducible promoter (for transient expression assays)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: siz1-2 mutant phenotyping; chlorate sensitivity assay; in vitro pull-down; yeast two-hybrid; in vitro sumoylation assay; in vivo transient expression and immunoprecipitation; K353R site-directed mutagenesis; nitrate reductase activity assay; specific activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of AtSIZ1-mediated activation is associated with dwarfism, early flowering, abnormal seed development, reduced NO, and impaired nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing NIA2 activity or its SUMO-dependent activation could improve nitrate assimilation efficiency, but manipulation should consider pleiotropic effects on growth, flowering, and defense pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both sumoylated and non-sumoylated NIA2 can form homodimers; protein stability is promoted by AtSIZ1. The paper treats ammonium rescue as evidence that defective nitrate reduction underlies the siz1 phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SP-RING-finger E3 SUMO ligase with SAP and Miz zinc-finger domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-translational activation and stabilization through SUMO conjugation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nitrogen assimilation via control of nitrate reduction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes sumoylation of NIA1 and NIA2, increasing their nitrate reductase activity and stability
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nutrient deficiency and environmental stresses are contextual roles mentioned; nitrate or ammonium did not significantly change AtSIZ1 transcript or protein levels in this study
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with SUMO machinery components AtSAE1b, AtSAE2, AtSCE1 and AtSUMO1 in SUMO conjugation reactions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator required for efficient nitrate assimilation because it activates and stabilizes the two nitrate reductases that drive nitrate-to-nitrite reduction
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen source for amino-acid pool and plant growth/development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtSIZ1 positively controls nitrogen assimilation; loss of AtSIZ1 decreases NR activity, lowers nitrogen and NO levels, raises nitrate, and causes growth and reproductive defects rescued by ammonium.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSIZ1 is the E3 SUMO ligase that directly interacts with NIA1 and NIA2 and promotes their sumoylation. 2. AtSIZ1-mediated sumoylation dramatically increases nitrate reductase activity and contributes to NR protein stability. 3. siz1-2 mutants show low nitrogen, high nitrate, low NO, dwarfism, early flowering, abnormal seed development, and these defects are largely rescued by ammonium. 4. AtSIZ1 transcript and protein abundance are not substantially changed by nitrate or ammonium treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in almost all plant tissues (as stated in Discussion).
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly; transcript and protein levels were examined in leaves and roots and were not significantly altered by nitrate or ammonium treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NIA1; NIA2; AtSAE1b; AtSAE2; AtSCE1; AtSUMO1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ammonium versus nitrate supply; cycloheximide treatment; pathogen infection with Pseudomonas syringae DG3
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: siz1-2 shows dwarfism, early flowering, abnormal seed development, altered salicylic acid accumulation, and enhanced bacterial disease resistance; boosting AtSIZ1 may therefore affect defense and development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AtSIZ1 is a candidate regulator for improving nitrogen use and nitrate assimilation efficiency, but deployment should consider strong pleiotropic effects on growth, flowering time, seed development, salicylic-acid signaling, and disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study provides the first biochemical evidence in plants that SUMO conjugation can directly stimulate catalytic activity of metabolic enzymes. AtSIZ1 effect is post-translational rather than transcriptional because nitrate-assimilation gene transcripts were not repressed in siz1-2.
